speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Take thy lute, wench. My soul grows sad with troubles.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Sing, and disperse ’em if thou canst. Leave working.

speaker: WOMAN
text: Orpheus with his lute made trees

speaker: WOMAN
text: And the mountaintops that freeze

speaker: WOMAN
text: Bow themselves when he did sing.

speaker: WOMAN
text: To his music plants and flowers

speaker: WOMAN
text: Ever sprung, as sun and showers

speaker: WOMAN
text: There had made a lasting spring.

speaker: WOMAN
text: Everything that heard him play,

speaker: WOMAN
text: Even the billows of the sea,

speaker: WOMAN
text: Hung their heads and then lay by.

speaker: WOMAN
text: In sweet music is such art,

speaker: WOMAN
text: Killing care and grief of heart

speaker: WOMAN
text: Fall asleep or, hearing, die.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: How now?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: An ’t please your Grace, the two great cardinals

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Wait in the presence.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Would they speak with me?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: They willed me say so, madam.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Pray their Graces

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To come near.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: What can be their business

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: With me, a poor weak woman, fall’n from favor?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I do not like their coming, now I think on ’t.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: They should be good men, their affairs as righteous.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: But all hoods make not monks.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Peace to your Highness.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Your Graces find me here part of a housewife;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I would be all, against the worst may happen.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: What are your pleasures with me, reverend lords?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: May it please you, noble madam, to withdraw

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Into your private chamber, we shall give you

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The full cause of our coming.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Speak it here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: There’s nothing I have done yet, o’ my conscience,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Deserves a corner. Would all other women

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Could speak this with as free a soul as I do.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: My lords, I care not, so much I am happy

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Above a number, if my actions

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Were tried by ev’ry tongue, ev’ry eye saw ’em,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Envy and base opinion set against ’em,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I know my life so even. If your business

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Seek me out, and that way I am wife in,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Out with it boldly. Truth loves open dealing.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Tanta est erga te mentis integritas, regina

speaker: WOLSEY
text: serenissima—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: O, good my lord, no Latin!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I am not such a truant since my coming

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: As not to know the language I have lived in.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: A strange tongue makes my cause more strange, suspicious.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Pray speak in English. Here are some will thank you,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: If you speak truth, for their poor mistress’ sake.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Believe me, she has had much wrong. Lord Cardinal,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The willing’st sin I ever yet committed

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: May be absolved in English.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Noble lady,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I am sorry my integrity should breed—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And service to his Majesty and you—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: So deep suspicion, where all faith was meant.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: We come not by the way of accusation,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: To taint that honor every good tongue blesses,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Nor to betray you any way to sorrow—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You have too much, good lady—but to know

speaker: WOLSEY
text: How you stand minded in the weighty difference

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Between the King and you, and to deliver,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Like free and honest men, our just opinions

speaker: WOLSEY
text: And comforts to your cause.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Most honored madam,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: My Lord of York, out of his noble nature,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Zeal, and obedience he still bore your Grace,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Forgetting, like a good man, your late censure

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Both of his truth and him—which was too far—

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Offers, as I do, in a sign of peace,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: His service and his counsel.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To betray me.—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: My lords, I thank you both for your good wills.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You speak like honest men; pray God you prove so.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: But how to make you suddenly an answer

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In such a point of weight, so near mine honor—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: More near my life, I fear—with my weak wit,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And to such men of gravity and learning,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In truth I know not. I was set at work

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Among my maids, full little, God knows, looking

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Either for such men or such business.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: For her sake that I have been—for I feel

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The last fit of my greatness—good your Graces,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Let me have time and counsel for my cause.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Alas, I am a woman friendless, hopeless.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Madam, you wrong the King’s love with these fears;

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Your hopes and friends are infinite.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In England

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: But little for my profit. Can you think, lords,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: That any Englishman dare give me counsel,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Or be a known friend, ’gainst his Highness’ pleasure,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Though he be grown so desperate to be honest,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And live a subject? Nay, forsooth. My friends,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: They that must weigh out my afflictions,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: They that my trust must grow to, live not here.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: They are, as all my other comforts, far hence

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: In mine own country, lords.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: I would your Grace

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Would leave your griefs and take my counsel.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: How, sir?

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Put your main cause into the King’s protection.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: He’s loving and most gracious. ’Twill be much

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Both for your honor better and your cause,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: For if the trial of the law o’ertake you,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: You’ll part away disgraced.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: He tells you rightly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You tell me what you wish for both: my ruin.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Is this your Christian counsel? Out upon you!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Heaven is above all yet; there sits a judge

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: That no king can corrupt.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Your rage mistakes us.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The more shame for you! Holy men I thought you,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Upon my soul, two reverend cardinal virtues;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: But cardinal sins and hollow hearts I fear you.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Mend ’em, for shame, my lords. Is this your comfort?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The cordial that you bring a wretched lady,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: A woman lost among you, laughed at, scorned?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I will not wish you half my miseries;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I have more charity. But say I warned you:

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Take heed, for heaven’s sake, take heed, lest at once

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: The burden of my sorrows fall upon you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Madam, this is a mere distraction.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You turn the good we offer into envy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You turn me into nothing! Woe upon you

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And all such false professors. Would you have me—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: If you have any justice, any pity,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: If you be anything but churchmen’s habits—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Put my sick cause into his hands that hates me?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Alas, has banished me his bed already,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: His love, too, long ago. I am old, my lords,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And all the fellowship I hold now with him

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Is only my obedience. What can happen

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To me above this wretchedness? All your studies

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Make me a curse like this.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Your fears are worse.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Have I lived thus long—let me speak myself,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Since virtue finds no friends—a wife, a true one—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: A woman, I dare say without vainglory,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Never yet branded with suspicion—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Have I with all my full affections

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Still met the King, loved him next heav’n, obeyed him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Been, out of fondness, superstitious to him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Almost forgot my prayers to content him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And am I thus rewarded? ’Tis not well, lords.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Bring me a constant woman to her husband,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: One that ne’er dreamed a joy beyond his pleasure,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: And to that woman, when she has done most,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Yet will I add an honor: a great patience.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Madam, you wander from the good we aim at.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: My lord, I dare not make myself so guilty

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To give up willingly that noble title

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Your master wed me to. Nothing but death

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Shall e’er divorce my dignities.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Pray hear me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Would I had never trod this English earth

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Or felt the flatteries that grow upon it!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You have angels’ faces, but heaven knows your hearts.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: What will become of me now, wretched lady?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I am the most unhappy woman living.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text:
To
                 
                her
                 
                Women
                .
                 
              Alas, poor wenches, where are now your fortunes?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Shipwracked upon a kingdom where no pity,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: No friends, no hope, no kindred weep for me,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Almost no grave allowed me, like the lily

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: That once was mistress of the field and flourished,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: I’ll hang my head and perish.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: If your Grace

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Could but be brought to know our ends are honest,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You’d feel more comfort. Why should we, good lady,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Upon what cause, wrong you? Alas, our places,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The way of our profession, is against it.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: We are to cure such sorrows, not to sow ’em.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: For goodness’ sake, consider what you do,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: How you may hurt yourself, ay, utterly

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Grow from the King’s acquaintance by this carriage.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The hearts of princes kiss obedience,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: So much they love it. But to stubborn spirits

speaker: WOLSEY
text: They swell and grow as terrible as storms.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I know you have a gentle, noble temper,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A soul as even as a calm. Pray think us

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Those we profess: peacemakers, friends, and servants.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Madam, you’ll find it so. You wrong your virtues

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: With these weak women’s fears. A noble spirit,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: As yours was put into you, ever casts

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Such doubts, as false coin, from it. The King loves you;

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Beware you lose it not. For us, if you please

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: To trust us in your business, we are ready

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: To use our utmost studies in your service.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Do what you will, my lords, and pray forgive me

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: If I have used myself unmannerly.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: You know I am a woman, lacking wit

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: To make a seemly answer to such persons.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Pray do my service to his Majesty.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: He has my heart yet and shall have my prayers

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: While I shall have my life. Come, reverend fathers,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: Bestow your counsels on me. She now begs

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: That little thought, when she set footing here,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              KATHERINE
text: She should have bought her dignities so dear.
